records.ety: [F. dgrader, LL. degradare, fr. L. de- + gradus step, degree. See Grade, and cf. Degree.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Degraded; p. pr. & vb. n. Degrading.]

defs: To reduce from a higher to a lower rank or degree; to lower in rank; to deprive of office or dignity; to strip of honors; as, to degrade a nobleman, or a general officer.
quotes: Prynne was sentenced by the Star Chamber Court to be degraded from the bar.

defs: To reduce in estimation, character, or reputation; to lessen the value of; to lower the physical, moral, or intellectual character of; to debase; to bring shame or contempt upon; to disgrace; as, vice degrades a man.
quotes: O miserable mankind, to what fall Degraded, to what wretched state reserved!
Yet time ennobles or degrades each line.
Her pride . . . struggled hard against this degrading passion.

defs: To reduce in altitude or magnitude, as hills and mountains; to wear down.
To degenerate; to pass from a higher to a lower type of structure; as, a family of plants or animals degrades through this or that genus or group of genera.
